items.title: Logger naming
items.avoid: Using the root logger directly everywhere.
items.why: Named loggers can be filtered and leveled per component.
items.example: `getLogger(__name__)` in `ragapp/chunker.py` → logger name `ragapp.chunker`. Operators can set `LOG_LEVEL_ragapp.chunker=DEBUG` to crank just that module.

items.title: Configuration ownership
items.avoid: Calling basicConfig or adding handlers inside library code.
items.prefer: Let the application configure logging once at startup; libraries only emit.
items.why: Prevents duplicate handlers and forced output for consumers.
items.example: Application `main.py`: `logging.basicConfig(...)` (once). Library: never. Add `logging.getLogger(__name__).addHandler(logging.NullHandler())` in `__init__.py` if you want to silence orphan warnings.

items.title: Sensitive data
items.avoid: Logging prompts, responses, tokens, or keys verbatim.
items.prefer: Log ids, counts and redacted snippets.
items.why: Logs are retained and broadly accessible.
items.example: `log.info('embedded prompt %s tokens=%d', pid, n)` — searchable, no PII. Not `log.info('embedded prompt: %s', prompt)` — leaks user data into long-term storage.

items.title: Choosing levels
items.avoid: Logging everything at INFO or ERROR indiscriminately.
items.prefer: DEBUG for detail, INFO for milestones, WARNING for recoverable issues, ERROR/CRITICAL for failures.
items.why: Meaningful levels make logs filterable and alertable.
items.example: DEBUG: 'embedding chunk 17'. INFO: 'ingested 1234 docs'. WARNING: 'retry 3/5 for embedding API'. ERROR: 'failed to write index'. CRITICAL: 'data corruption detected'.
